title: Invalid JSON generated by json_group_array()
description:
drh added on 2016-01-30 13:56:13:
The first column in the output of the following query contains invalid
JSON.  The "abc" string value is not quoted.  The output is
[1,abc] instead of [1,"abc"]
CREATE TABLE t1(x);
INSERT INTO t1 VALUES(1),('abc');
SELECT
   json_group_array(x),
   json_group_array(json_object('x',x))
  FROM t1;
This problem was reported by Marien Zwart on the SQLite Users
mailing list.
drh added on 2016-01-30 14:54:49:
The underlying problem was that the subtype information for a VDBE register
was not being cleared when that register was overwritten and reused, which
cause cause the subtype from a prior json_object() call to leak into the
parameter of the json_group_array() call.  Upgraded the severity to Severe
since this problem affects all users of sqlite3_value_subtype() and
sqlite3_result_subtype().
key_bug_features: Invalid JSON output from json_group_array() function
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(x INTEGER, y TEXT, z REAL);
INSERT INTO t1 VALUES(1, 'abc', 3.14), (NULL, 'def', NULL), (2, NULL, 2.71);
CREATE TABLE t2(a INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, b TEXT NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO t2 SELECT x, json_group_array(y) FROM t1 GROUP BY x;
SELECT json_group_array(json_object('x', x, 'y', y)), json_group_array(json_object('z', z)) FROM t1;
